title: OSCP Black Book Inspired Workflow
description: Local, checklist-driven workflow centered on passive recon, active scanning, and documentation.

items.title: Validate target scope
items.guide_text: Confirm the target hostname, IP, or CIDR is within your authorized lab or engagement scope before running any active tools.
items.example_command: Document scope and validate target input before execution.

items.title: Discover subdomains
items.guide_text: Use passive sources to identify visible subdomains before deeper validation.

items.title: Resolve DNS records
items.guide_text: Resolve A, AAAA, CNAME, NS, MX, and TXT records for the scoped domain and store them as normalized findings.

items.title: Run host discovery
items.guide_text: Identify responsive hosts first and capture findings as structured data.

items.title: Probe live web services
items.guide_text: Check reachable HTTP(S) services, titles, status codes, IPs, and technology fingerprints for scoped targets.

items.title: Run chained web triage
items.guide_text: Discover subdomains, verify live web services, and run nuclei template checks against the reachable targets.

items.title: Run service enumeration
items.guide_text: Enumerate open services and versions on in-scope hosts and store parsed output.

items.title: Record findings and next actions
items.guide_text: Capture notes, exposed services, and follow-up validation tasks in encrypted local storage.
